data.content: I can see the project has a `src/routes.tsx` file which is likely the main routing file. Let me read the key routing-related files:

Now I have all the key pieces. Here's a comprehensive explanation of how routing is organized in this React admin app:

---

## Routing Architecture

The routing is defined in **`src/routes.tsx`** and uses **React Router v6** (`react-router-dom`). The router is mounted in `src/main.tsx` inside a `<BrowserRouter>`.

### Two Top-Level Layout Branches

The `<Routes>` tree splits into **two layout branches**:

```
<Routes>
  ├── /dashboard   →  DashboardLayout
  └── /            →  LogoOnlyLayout
</Routes>
```

---

### 1. Dashboard Layout (`/dashboard`)

The `DashboardLayout` component (`src/layouts/dashboard/index.tsx`) provides the **full admin shell** — a sidebar (`DashboardSidebar`), a top navbar (`DashboardNavbar`), and a main content area that renders child routes via `<Outlet />`.

| Path | Component | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `/dashboard` | `<Navigate to="/dashboard/app" replace />` | **Redirect** — bare `/dashboard` immediately redirects to `/dashboard/app` |
| `/dashboard/app` | `<DashboardApp />` | Main dashboard/home view |
| `/dashboard/user` | `<User />` | User management page |
| `/dashboard/products` | `<Products />` | Products page |
| `/dashboard/blog` | `<Blog />` | Blog page |

All child routes are **eagerly loaded** (standard synchronous imports, no lazy loading).

---

### 2. Logo-Only Layout (`/`) — Auth Pages & Fallbacks

The `LogoOnlyLayout` (`src/layouts/LogoOnlyLayout.tsx`) is a **minimal shell** — just a logo in the top-left corner and the page content via `<Outlet />`. No sidebar or navbar.

| Path | Component | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `/login` | `<Login />` | Login page |
| `/register` | `<Register />` | Registration page |
| `/404` | `<NotFound />` (Page404) | Explicit 404 page |
| `/` (empty) | `<Navigate to="/dashboard" />` | **Root redirect** — visiting `/` sends you straight to `/dashboard` |
| `*` (catch-all) | `<Navigate to="/404" />` | **Fallback** — any unmatched path under `/` redirects to `/404` |

---

### 3. Redirect Summary

| From | To | Mechanism |
|---|---|---|
| `/` | `/dashboard` | `<Navigate>` (index route of `/`) |
| `/dashboard` | `/dashboard/app` | `<Navigate>` with `replace` (index route of `/dashboard`) |
| Any unknown path | `/404` | `<Navigate>` from catch-all `*` route, then rendered by `<NotFound />` |

The `replace` on `/dashboard → /dashboard/app` prevents the bare `/dashboard` from cluttering browser history.

---

### 4. 404 / Not Found Behavior

The `Page404` component (`src/pages/Page404.tsx`) renders:
- A "Sorry, page not found!" heading with a fade-in animation (Framer Motion).
- A 404 illustration SVG.
- A **"Go to Home"** button that links back to `/`.

Any route that doesn't match `/dashboard/*`, `/login`, `/register`, or `/404` itself will hit the `*` catch-all, redirect to `/404`, and display this page — all rendered within the `LogoOnlyLayout` (logo-only chrome, no dashboard sidebar).

---

### 5. Auth-Related Pages Note

There is also an unused `AuthLayout` component in `src/layouts/AuthLayout.tsx` (not referenced in the router). The actual auth pages (`Login`, `Register`) are rendered under the `LogoOnlyLayout`. There is **no route guard or protected-route wrapper** in this routing setup — the routes are purely declarative with no authentication checks gating access to `/dashboard/*`.
